frontier.hypothesis: A per-file cc1 flag exists that alters sched2's rank_for_schedule tie-break behavior on the T-14 pair (sll a0<<2 vs sll v0<<2, both pri=2) without disturbing byte-identical build for other functions in system.c, breaking the T-14 INSN_LUID tie s17 named as the sole remaining tie-break axis.
frontier.mechanism: s6/s15/s17 CONFIRMED the T-14 tie is decided by INSN_LUID from sched2's own backward walk, not by expand-time UID or opcode. GCC 2.7.2 has -f flags governing scheduler passes (-fno-schedule-insns2 disables sched2 entirely — probably regresses other functions but could be per-file). Other candidates: -fno-cse-follow-jumps, -fno-move-loop-invariants, -fno-thread-jumps, -fno-schedule-insns (pre-RA sched, could shift ready-list backward-walk seed). Any flag that alters rank_for_schedule's forward/backward walk seed OR alters sched2's tie-break priority ordering could shift the LUID landscape.
frontier.next_probe: Structural/forensics session: (a) enumerate GCC 2.7.2 -f/-fno-* flags via `tools/gcc-2.7.2/build/cc1 --help` output; (b) write a per-file cc1 wrapper shim in tmp/ that applies the flag ONLY to system.c compilation; (c) measure sandbox under each candidate flag with candidate.c spliced; (d) simultaneously verify no other function in system.c regresses under the flag (spot-check sandbox on 2-3 sibling fns from queue). Flags that pass both gates are per-file compile-flag levers, a genuine new axis not yet catalogued.

As synthesis for THIS function: block further marionation_Exec permuter/rederive/structural sessions until twin gets its own session — the same three attractor classes will surface repeatedly otherwise (s19 confirmed convergence). Read pre-merge commits aa8cad24 cd65f9c2 e5aa2dd4 f4bc8e67 df749bfd 622620cb BEFORE the twin session for the allocation equation.

frontier.mechanism: s19 SYNTHESIS finding: the 4 sampled basins (vT40/find105/z07/w05) all converge on the same closed attractor set — but they share tbase/staging structure. s18v02 (check-region as structured if/else with returns, folded to identical post-jump.c CFG per s18 evidence) is the ONE known masked-4 spelling with a genuinely different upstream CFG. The 5 other unsampled masked-4 chassis (s11 u10 interleaved-alt, s11 w03 pp-after-loads, s11 w10 s32-cast deref, s17 a4 4*t0 LHS-const-mult, s17 a5 t0*(2*2)) are variations on the vT40 staging pattern — likely convergent. s18v02 is the outlier worth sampling.

judge_constraints: FINAL owner ruling 2026-07-06 (do-while-zero-exception.md): do-while(0) sanctioned for ANY codegen effect incl. RA weighting, mandatory inline FAKE annotation; nested wraps additionally need a single-level-insufficient justification. The interim not-sanctioned ruling is superseded.
